fix: prevent concurrent pipeline launches from bypassing queue mode (#5078)

* fix: prevent concurrent pipeline launches from bypassing queue mode

Two bugs allowed multiple pipelines to launch simultaneously even when
queue mode was enabled for a project:

Bug 1 — Race condition in execute_pipeline_launch():
The queue gate (count_active_pipelines_for_project check) ran after ~150
lines of async work including GitHub API calls for issue creation and
sub-issue creation. When multiple pipelines were launched concurrently,
all coroutines reached the queue gate before any had registered their
pipeline state in the L1 cache. Each saw active_count == 0 and bypassed
the queue, resulting in all pipelines starting simultaneously.

Bug 2 — Self-counting in the queue gate:
set_pipeline_state() was called with queued=False BEFORE the queue gate
check. When count_active_pipelines_for_project() ran, it counted the
pipeline itself as active, causing active_count >= 1 even when no OTHER
pipeline was running. This meant a sole pipeline would queue itself.

Fix:
- Add per-project asyncio.Lock (get_project_launch_lock) to serialise
  the queue-gate check-and-register sequence. Concurrent launches for
  the same project now acquire this lock, ensuring only one coroutine
  evaluates the active count at a time.

- Add exclude_issue parameter to count_active_pipelines_for_project()
  so a pipeline does not count itself when deciding whether to queue.

- Move pipeline state registration inside the lock, with the correct
  queued flag set atomically with the count check.

- Apply the same lock in _dequeue_next_pipeline() to prevent races
  between dequeue and concurrent new launches.

Files changed:
- pipeline_state_store.py: Added get_project_launch_lock() and
  exclude_issue param to count_active_pipelines_for_project()
- transitions.py: Re-export get_project_launch_lock
- workflow_orchestrator/__init__.py: Re-export get_project_launch_lock
- pipelines.py: Restructured execute_pipeline_launch() queue gate
  to use lock + exclude_issue
- copilot_polling/pipeline.py: _dequeue_next_pipeline() uses lock
  and checks active_count before dequeuing
- tests/unit/test_queue_mode.py: Added 8 tests covering exclude_issue,
  lock identity/serialisation, and concurrent launch simulation

Reported issue: 4 parent issues (#5011, #5021, #5033, #5050) all
launched simultaneously despite queue mode being enabled.

Add adaptive inscribed-sphere cache to accelerate Inside(p) (#197)

* Add adaptive inscribed-sphere cache to accelerate Inside(p)

Every DistanceToOut(p) call already returns a provably inscribed sphere
B(p, d) where d is the BVH lower-bound safety distance. Cache these spheres
per-thread and use them to short-circuit Inside(p) before the expensive
BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier::Perform call.

Changes:
- Add InscribedSphere{centre, radius} and SphereCacheData structs.
- Seed fInitialSpheres at construction from up to 15 interior candidate
  points (AABB centre, 6 face midpoints, 8 octant centres) using a local
  BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier for inside-check and BVHLowerBoundDistance
  for the radius. Sorted descending by radius.
- Add G4Cache<SphereCacheData> fSphereCache (thread-local, same pattern
  as fClassifierCache). GetOrInitSphereCache() copies fInitialSpheres on
  first access per thread or after a shape update.
- TryInsertSphere(centre, d): accepts a new sphere only if it is not fully
  dominated by an existing entry (dominance check avoids sqrt via squaring),
  and the cache is bounded to kMaxInscribedSpheres=64 entries sorted
  descending by radius.
- Inside(p): after the AABB early-out, scan the sphere cache before calling
  OCCT. A hit returns kInside immediately (~640 flops worst case vs the
  ~88 000 flops of BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier for a 6-face box).
- DistanceToOut(p): feeds TryInsertSphere after computing the result so
  coverage grows automatically during tracking.
- Destructor: clears fSphereCache.Get().spheres alongside the existing
  classifier/intersector reset.

For g4box-box-20x30x40-v1 the AABB centre seed sphere has radius ≈10 mm,
immediately covering 17% of the interior. After a few dozen tracking steps
the cache reaches near-100% hit rate and the BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier is
never called for interior points.

No new correctness risk: every cached sphere radius d satisfies
d ≤ dist_to_surface(centre) by construction, so no false kInside can occur.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add EntityTypeName overrides to all compiled entities, fix 141 test failures (#1535)

The upstream GetType() elimination commit (f34ea2e) changed dictionary keys
from Type to string (EntityTypeName) across SearchIndexing, ReportExecutor,
CalculatedFieldService, PrincipalAuthorizationPolicy, and DataQueryEvaluator.

EntityTypeName returns empty string for entities created via new T() because
Schema is only set by the Handlers.Create() factory wrapper. This broke all
systems relying on entity name lookups.

Fix: add compile-time EntityTypeName overrides to all 68 compiled entity
classes (production + test), matching their [DataEntity] attribute names.
No reflection, no GetType() — fully explicit, zero-cost property overrides.

Additional fixes:
- DataQueryEvaluator: infer memberType from stored value when Schema absent
- SystemPrincipal: add EntityTypeName override (extends User, needs own name)
- ToolRegistryTests: update assertion for ex.Message (was ex.GetType().Name)
- ControlPlaneObservabilityTests: same assertion update for error hook

Before: 154 failures (106 Data, 47 Host, 1 Intelligence)
After:   13 failures (1 Data, 12 Host) — all pre-existing test isolation issues

feat(agent-shell): preToolUse hook support for policy-based command blocking (#317)

* feat(agent-shell): add PolicyConfig, HooksConfig, and PolicyDecisionEvent schemas

## Release Overview: `@lousy-agents/agent-shell`

**Policy-based command blocking for AI coding agents (foundation)**

This release adds the core infrastructure for intercepting and evaluating terminal commands before AI agents execute them via GitHub Copilot's `preToolUse` hook.

### What's New

- **Policy configuration schema** — Define allow/deny rules for commands using exact match or glob patterns (`.github/hooks/agent-shell/policy.json`)
- **Repository root discovery** — Automatically locates config files relative to git root, not cwd
- **Policy evaluation engine** — Linear-time glob matcher (no regex backtracking vulnerabilities)
- **`policy-check` CLI mode** — Reads hook input from stdin, evaluates against policy, returns JSON decision
- **Fail-closed by default** — Invalid config or malformed input blocks execution
- **Telemetry for policy decisions** — Audit trail of allowed/denied commands

### Not Yet Complete

This is foundational work. Full feature requires follow-up for:
- Log query integration for policy events
- README/usage documentation  
- `copilot-setup` hook provisioning command

### Example Policy

```json
{
  "deny": ["rm -rf *", "npm publish*"],
  "allow": ["npm test", "npm run build"]
}
```

fix(ralph): route by last review comment type, not any approval

Previously, determine_mode() checked 'APPROVED > 0' to pick merge mode.
This caused an infinite loop: merge mode posts REQUEST_CHANGES when CI
fails, but the next iteration still saw APPROVED > 0 and routed back to
merge, which posted another REQUEST_CHANGES, forever.

Fix: look at the *last* RALPH-REVIEW comment type instead:
- Last comment is APPROVED → merge
- Last comment is REQUEST_CHANGES → fix (no new commits) or
  review-round2/merge (new commits after RC)

Also: if new commits exist after the last RC *and* the PR has already
received an APPROVED, skip force-approve and go straight to merge.
The merge step re-checks CI anyway.

commit.message:
perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop + bbox prefilter (#215)

* perf: replace IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector with per-face intersector loop

Replace the single IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector::Perform call (33.5%
of total instructions in the B4c callgrind profile, 5,591M instructions)
with a custom per-face loop over IntCurvesFace_Intersector objects.

Two sources of overhead are eliminated:

1. NCollection_Sequence heap management (~31% of total): the ShapeIntersector
   accumulates results in a heap-allocated NCollection_Sequence (ClearSeq,
   Find, AppendPoint, malloc/free).  The per-face loop instead tracks the
   closest hit in a stack-allocated scalar.

2. All-face intersection without prefiltering (~2%): per-face intersectors
   are gated behind Bnd_Box::IsOut(gp_Lin), rejecting faces whose AABB the
   ray misses in O(1).

Implementation details:

- IntersectorCache now holds std::vector<std::unique_ptr<IntCurvesFace_Intersector>>
  (one per face, heap-allocated to avoid movability requirements) and
  std::vector<Bnd_Box> expandedBoxes (each face box enlarged by
  IntersectionTolerance() to prevent IsOut false positives for zero-thickness
  planar face AABBs).

- GetOrCreateIntersector() rebuilds both vectors on generation mismatch.

- DistanceToOut no longer needs the fFaceAdaptorIndex hash-map lookup:
  the loop tracks minFaceIdx directly, giving O(1) access to the cached
  BRepAdaptor_Surface for normal computation.

- DistanceToOut normal condition updated to accept TopAbs_IN || TopAbs_ON,
  fixing pre-existing failures for sphere/cylinder seam hit points that
  IntCurvesFace_Intersector correctly classifies as ON (not IN).

Smart mouse mode: defer disable until click above viewport (#328)

In smart mode, mouse capture was disabled whenever the mouse moved above
the viewport — too aggressive, preventing hover/movement above the
prompt without losing capture. The intended trigger is user intent to
interact with terminal content above (e.g. text selection), which only a
click signals.

## Changes

- **`src/app/mod.rs`**: In the smart mode `on_mouse` handler, gate the
`disable()` call on `MouseEventKind::Down(_)`. Events above the viewport
still return `false` (skipped by flyline) but no longer tear down
capture on mere movement.

```rust
if mouse.row < self.last_viewport_top {
    // Only disable on an actual click above the viewport
    if matches!(mouse.kind, MouseEventKind::Down(_)) {
        self.mouse_state.disable("smart mode: click above the viewport");
    }
    self.last_mouse_over_cell = None;
    return false;
}
```

- **`src/settings.rs`** / **`README.md`**: Updated `MouseMode::Smart`
doc and both README occurrences — "mouse moves above the viewport" →
"user clicks above the viewport".

Remove MyApp.slnx — fixes ambiguous solution file error

Having both MyApp.sln and MyApp.slnx in the same directory causes
MSBuild to error with MSB1011 when running 'dotnet build' or
'dotnet test' without specifying a file explicitly.

MyApp.sln (traditional format) was added to fix C# DevKit language
server compatibility. Now that it exists, slnx is redundant and
the ambiguity causes a worse problem than the one it solved.

fix: Windows test failures in config command and agents compiler (#410)

Fix 7 pre-existing Windows CI test failures:

- test_config_command.py (5 failures): PermissionError [WinError 32] when
  TemporaryDirectory cleanup runs while CWD is still inside it. Windows
  holds a handle on the CWD directory. Fix: os.chdir() back to original
  dir inside try/finally before the TemporaryDirectory context exits.

- test_agents_compiler_coverage.py (2 failures): Path separator mismatch.
  relative_to() returns Path with OS-native separators (backslash on
  Windows), but assertions check for forward slashes. Fix: use .as_posix()
  in production code for consistent cross-platform summary output.

commit.message:
fix: persist initial task message and include all message types in export (#204)

Fixes two user-reported bugs with session messages.

## Bug 1: Initial task message lost on refresh
The user's initial task message was passed to `queueMessage()` but never
persisted to the database. On page refresh, the message disappeared from
the chat history.

**Fix:** Call `persistHumanMessage()` before `queueMessage()` so the
initial task is written to the messages table immediately.

## Bug 2: Session export missing message types
Session markdown export only captured agent-to-agent messages, missing
user messages, system messages, and incoming agent messages.

**Fix:** Include `fromRole` in message headers and capture all message
types (human, system, incoming) in the export query.

## Testing
- 4 new tests covering both fixes
- All existing tests pass
- TypeScript compiles clean

## Review
- Code review: ✅ Approved
- Critical review: ✅ Approved
- Readability review: ✅ Approved
